[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged attacker craft a signature with high-s malleability (v ^ 1, r, secp256k1.n - s) from a legitimately observed (v, r, s) under the precondition that a valid take has already been executed and the original signature is known, call take() with the malleable signature in ratifierData, violating the invariant that each unique signature byte-string authorizes at most one distinct authorization event, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer fill using a forged-but-valid ecrecover signature?
